model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Despite significant personnel losses—starting forward Matt Hodge's season-ending torn ACL and Zion Stanford's departure from the program—Villanova's overall statistical profile (KenPom #33, Torvik #36) and Big East pedigree give them a razor-thin edge in this classic 8/9 coin-flip. The Wildcats' adjusted efficiency margin of +19.94 and solid defense (AdjD 100.4, 37th nationally) should still be competitive even in a diminished state, though the shock loss to Georgetown in the Big East Tournament and the roster upheaval make this essentially a toss-up. Utah State's Mountain West credentials are respectable for a 9-seed, but Villanova's higher-conference experience and remaining talent barely tip the scales in their favor.

reasoningStep.title: West 8/9: Villanova vs Utah State
reasoningStep.summary: Villanova narrowly favored despite devastating personnel losses, as their Big East-tested metrics and defensive profile provide a slim edge in a true coin-flip 8/9 matchup.
evidence: Villanova ranks 33rd in KenPom (AdjEM +19.94, AdjO 120.4 at 42nd, AdjD 100.4) and 36th in Torvik with a 24-8 Big East record
Starting forward Matt Hodge suffered a season-ending torn ACL just weeks before the tournament
Junior Zion Stanford left the Villanova program before the NCAA Tournament
Villanova suffered a shock loss to Georgetown in the Big East Tournament, raising concerns about form and depth
Utah State ratings were unavailable but as a 9-seed from the Mountain West they project as a comparable opponent in the 35-55 range nationally
8/9 seed matchups historically split close to 50/50, and Villanova's roster losses narrow their statistical advantage to near-parity

result.text: Villanova rocked by devastating Matt Hodge injury as torn ACL ends his season Skip to main content About NCAA Basketball News March Madness ACC Basketball Big 12 Basketball Big Ten Basketball SEC Basketball Other Conferences Follow Us Share on X About NCAA Basketball News March Madness ACC Basketball Big 12 Basketball Big Ten Basketball SEC Basketball Other Conferences Other Conferences AAC Basketball Atlantic 10 Big East C-USA Missouri Valley Basketball MWC Pac-12 Baskeball WCC Basketball Share on X Villanova rocked by devastating Matt Hodge injury as torn ACL ends his season Villanova’s postseason push took a major hit Monday as starting forward Matt Hodge was ruled out for the remainder of the season with a torn ACL. The redshirt freshman had become a steady presence in the Wildcats’ rotation, and his absence leaves a difficult void just weeks before tournament play begins. By Rodney Knuppel | Share on Facebook Share on X Share on Reddit Copy Article Add us as a preferred source Villanova Wildcats forward Matt Hodge (33) | Bill Streicher-Imagn Images There are injuries that sting, and then there are injuries that take the air out of a season. --> There are injuries that sting, and then there are injuries that take the air out of a season. Villanova got the kind no team wants to hear about in March. --> Villanova got the kind no team wants to hear about in March. Redshirt freshman forward Matt Hodge suffered a torn ACL in his right knee during the second half of Saturday night’s loss at St. John's Red Storm. Further testing confirmed the diagnosis, and surgery will sideline him for the rest of the 2025-26 season. --> Redshirt freshman forward Matt Hodge suffered a torn ACL in his right knee during the second half of Saturday night’s loss at St. John's Red Storm. Further testing confirmed the diagnosis, and surgery will sideline him for the rest of the 2025-26 season. For the Villanova Wildcats , it is a devastating blow at the worst possible time. --> For the Villanova Wildcats , it is a devastating blow at the worst possible time. A season that finally felt like his --> A season that finally felt like his Hodge waited a long time for this year. --> Hodge waited a long time for this year. After sitting out last season because of an NCAA initial eligibility ruling, the Belgium native finally got his opportunity to contribute in a real way. He did not ease into it. He started all 29 regular-season games. --> After sitting out last season because of an NCAA initial eligibility ruling, the Belgium native finally got his opportunity to contribute in a real way. He did not ease into it. He started all 29 regular-season games. He averaged 9.2 points and 3.6 rebounds in nearly 28 minutes a night. He shot 45.2 percent from the field and a solid 36.8 percent from three. Those numbers tell part of the story. --> He averaged 9.2 points and 3.6 rebounds in nearly 28 minutes a night. Based on the data I've gathered, let me synthesize my analysis:

**What I know:**

**Villanova (8-seed):**
- Record: 24-8
- KenPom: #33, AdjEM +19.94, AdjO 120.4 (42nd), AdjD 100.4
- Torvik: #36, AdjOE 119.6 (44th), AdjDE 100.5 (37th)
- Conference: Big East
- **Critical injuries:** Starting forward Matt Hodge out for season (torn ACL). Zion Stanford left the program before the tournament. These are devastating losses heading into March.
- Lost to Georgetown in Big East Tournament (shock loss)
